tasks.title: Fix li-post.sh TELEGRAM_USER_ID variable passing
tasks.description: li-post.sh fails with NO_TOKEN even when token is valid. Root cause: TELEGRAM_USER_ID env var not exported to subprocess context when skill calls li-post.sh.
steps: Open /data/workspace/skills/linkedin-publish/SKILL.md
Find the bash block that calls li-post.sh
Add 'export TELEGRAM_USER_ID' before the call, OR use: TELEGRAM_USER_ID="$TELEGRAM_USER_ID" li-post.sh ...
Test with Catherine (uid 547748231)

tasks.title: Create Catherine's strategy.md and content-calendar.md
tasks.description: Catherine is missing strategy.md and content-calendar.md. Agent fails to read them on every session start (ENOENT). This limits content planning quality.
steps: Ask Catherine about her content goals, posting frequency, target audience
Create /data/workspace/social/linkedin/547748231/strategy.md with: goals, posting cadence, key messages, topic pillars
Create /data/workspace/social/linkedin/547748231/content-calendar.md with upcoming content ideas
Verify brain-push.sh pushes these to git (already fixed)

tasks.title: Fix duplicate message / NO_REPLY ghost responses
tasks.description: Every assistant response appears twice in Telegram with a NO_REPLY ghost in between. Likely an OpenClaw delivery confirmation timing issue.
steps: Review OpenClaw session delivery/ACK configuration
Check if there's a timeout setting for message delivery confirmation
Look at Telegram bot delivery confirmation logs
Consider adding deduplication at the session layer
Test fix with a new session

tasks.title: Configure web_search provider
tasks.description: web_search is disabled. Configure ZEROENTROPY_API_KEY or Brave/Perplexity search in OpenClaw config.
steps: Get a ZeroEntropy API key OR a Brave Search API key
Add to container environment: ZEROENTROPY_API_KEY=<key>
Or add search provider config to openclaw config
Test by asking agent to search for trending topics
files: docker-compose.yml or Kubernetes deployment env vars

tasks.title: Set up Rudolf's brand voice and strategy
tasks.description: Rudolf (373382939) is approved but has no brand voice profile or strategy. Next time he uses the bot, onboarding should be triggered.
steps: When Rudolf next messages the bot, run linkedin-onboarding skill
Create /data/workspace/social/linkedin/373382939/ directory
Set up profile.md and strategy.md for Rudolf

tasks.title: Fix LinkedIn token refresh — store refresh_token properly
tasks.description: Catherine's refresh_token is empty. When access_token expires (~Aug 29 2026), she'll need to manually reconnect. Fix li-auth.sh to capture and store refresh token.
steps: Check li-auth.sh OAuth exchange response parsing
Ensure refresh_token from LinkedIn OAuth response is stored
Add GATE1 auto-refresh: if token expires in <7 days, attempt refresh
Test refresh flow

tasks.title: Install browser/Playwright tool in container
tasks.description: Browser tool unavailable. Needed for JS-rendered pages (SPAs). This affects trend research and blog post scraping.
steps: Add Playwright to Dockerfile: RUN npx playwright install chromium --with-deps
Configure OpenClaw browser tool in openclaw config
Test with benchgen.com/blog pages
Note: increases container size significantly

tasks.title: Implement session continuity — auto-load last session summary on start
tasks.description: Sessions reset every few days, losing conversation context. Implement a protocol where agent reads previous session notes at the start of each session.
steps: Update log-export.sh to also write a per-user latest-session-summary.md
Update AGENTS.md to instruct agent: on first turn in a new session, read /data/workspace/logs/summaries/<user_id>-latest.md
Or: when a session resets, agent writes a handoff note to memory before reset

tasks.title: Deploy updated container image (v0.0.22)
tasks.description: brain-push.sh fix and log-export.sh additions need to be deployed. Current container is betedo/cbrain:0.0.21. New image with fixes is built but not deployed.
steps: Build new image: docker build -t betedo/cbrain:0.0.22 .
Push to registry: docker push betedo/cbrain:0.0.22
Update Kubernetes deployment to use 0.0.22
Verify brain-push.sh git sync works after deployment
Verify log-export.sh runs on schedule

tasks.title: Pending user: Catherine Miliutenko approval in approved-users.json
tasks.description: Catherine (547748231) appears in pending-users.json in the git repo as of 2026-07-06. But she's been using the bot since early July. The server's approved-users.json on the pod likely already has her (since GATE1=pass, IS_APPROVED=true in her sessions). However, the git-tracked approved-users.json does NOT have her yet. This could cause issues after a pod restart (git reset --hard would restore the old approved-users.json without Catherine).
steps: Add '547748231' to /data/workspace/social/approved-users.json
Remove her entry from pending-users.json
Verify brain-push.sh pushes this change to git
After next pod restart, confirm she still has access
